rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF TREVOR BRICE SPARKMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR TERMINATION OF GUARDIANSHIP of Person and Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. No notice was given to the paternal grandparents. Proposed Order must be submitted before the hearing. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/5/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Notice must include the language for remote appearance procedures, as required by LRSF 14.1 D.6. The paralegals attended hearings..? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 3/24/23. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B.] (The prayer has the incorrect amount of bond in paragraph 4; however, the appropriate amount of bond will be ordered.) NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIEN HEDQUIST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/5/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Notice must include the language for remote appearance procedures, as required by LRSF 14.1 D.6. This Court requires distributions to a minor exceeding the amount under Probate Code Sect. 3401 either to be distributed to a Court-appointed guardian of the estate or deposited into a California FDIC insured blocked account. [LRSF 14.35 E.] The identity and address of the financial institution must be set forth in a verified pleading. If the statutory fee is based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] 2% of $16,084.89 = $321.70, not $321.97. Attorney costs are not fully itemized, showing each payee and description of the cost. What was sent via messenger to the court on 12/21/21, 12/29/21, and 1/13/22? To whom was the exemplification fee paid on 3/31/22? Please explain the $16,066.79 in "Estate Execution Expence [sic]" subtracted from this estate's ½ of the sale proceeds. Was there an order authorizing the $1,125.00 retainer and $6,900.00 fee paid to New York Counsel, respectively, on 2/26/22 and 3/28/22? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 3/24/23. (NB: Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir OR BENEFICIARY HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. The will was lodged on 12/29/21 but not admitted until 1/18/22.A petition for final distribution must also state the DATE on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.36 I.2.]) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THEODORE P. GLAZA TRUST DATED APRIL 14, 2014
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions To Enforce The No Contest Clause Against Matthew A. Guerra, To Ascertain Beneficiaries Of The Theodore P. Glaza Trust, And To Compel Trust Information
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/5/23. STILL no proposed order has been submitted or received by the assigned examiner as of 2/8/23. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] The Court will not hear a matter until a proposed order has been submitted to the examiner. If the petition is not withdrawn (informally by emailing the assigned examiner with a cc to the other party) or dismissed (formally by filing a Request for Dismissal) and no proposed order is received by the assigned examiner by 3/24/23, an appearance will be required on 4/5/23 for the Court to determine if the petition should be taken off calendar. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM COLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/28/23 at Counsel's request. (No earlier date available.) Other issues posted for the 1/11/23 hearing have not yet been addressed: (1) no proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.]; (2) no complete Acknowledgment of Duties & Liabilities; (3) no supplement to the petition, answering item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition; and (4) no proof of service of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate. Mailed notice must include the language for remote appearance procedures, as required by LRSF 14.1.D.6. New deadline for providing the examiner with the proposed order and courtesy copies of any new papers filed to address the issues is 3/17/23.
